statement.p: Simplify this expression, and if applicable, write the restricted domain.

solution.p: To simply the complex fraction \frac{{\frac{1}{r+2}+\frac{2}{r-2}}}{{1-\frac{1}{r-2}}}, we multiple the numerator and denominator with the common denominator of {\frac{1}{r+2}+\frac{2}{r-2}} and {1-\frac{1}{r-2}}, which is {\left(r+2\right)\mathopen{}\left(r-2\right)}.
Since r-2 was canceled, r \ne 2 is a lost domain condition.
Although r+2 was also canceled, r \ne -2 is not a lost domain condition because it is implied in the solution.
